content.problemStatement:
You are given an undirected weighted tree of NNN nodes, numbered from 111 to NNN.

The cost of a path is defined as the **bitwise AND** of the weights of the edges on that path. The cost of the path having only one node is, by default, 000.

You are given QQQ queries. In each query, you will be given a node xxx. For each query, your task is to find two nodes uuu and vvv, such that


The node xxx lies on the simple path from node uuu to node vvv.
The cost of this path is greater than 000.
The number of nodes on this path is **maximum**
If there are multiple answers, print any. If there exists no answer, print "−1−1-1 -1−1−1" (without quotes).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN, denoting the number of nodes in the tree.
The next N−1N-1N−1 lines contains three space-separated integers uuu, vvv and www, denoting an undirected edge between node uuu and node vvv with weight www.
The next line contains an integer QQQ denoting the number of queries.
The next QQQ lines contain an integer xxx denoting the query node.




Output Format
For each test case, output QQQ lines containing two space-separated integers, the answer to that query.
constraints: The sum of NNN and QQQ over all test cases won't exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first query, the bitwise AND of the simple path from node 555 to node 111 is (6&4&5)(6 \& 4 \& 5)(6&4&5) which is 444.
In the third query, there is no possible answer.
